headline: JPMorgan Just Hiked Hewlett Packard Enterprise Price Target to $37: Memory Reversal Powers IT Hardware Rally
summary: JPMorgan just raised its price target on Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) to $37 from $27, maintaining an Overweight rating. The firm tied the call to a reversal of memory-related concerns that has powered the recent rally in the IT hardware group. For prudent investors, the price target raise signals that Wall Street sees more room ... JPMorgan Just Hiked Hewlett Packard Enterprise Price Target to $37: Memory Reversal Powers IT Hardware Rally

headline: JPMorgan Hikes Dell Price Target to $280: Memory Concerns Fade as AI Server Story Powers On
summary: Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) just got another bullish nod from Wall Street. JPMorgan raised its price target on Dell Technologies to $280 from $205, keeping an Overweight rating, arguing that memory cost headwinds have eased even as the AI server franchise continues to scale. headline: Trump went big on tech stocks in first quarter of 2026, new filings show
summary: President Trump bought shares of Amazon, Meta, Oracle, Broadcom, Motorola and Dell worth millions, new ethics disclosure filings show.

headline: Trump Bought the Software Stock Slump—and More Revelations From His Trades
summary: President Donald Trump has been busy in the stock market.  Trump disclosed purchases and sales of hundreds of millions of dollars worth of U.S. stocks in the first quarter, according to a disclosure released by the U.S. Office of Government Ethics.  Trump purchased between $1 million and $5 million worth of  ServiceNow Adobe Workday  on Feb. 10, the disclosure revealed.
